title: Cylinder volume calculator (cm³ and liters)
shortDescription: Calculate the volume of a cylinder from its radius and height.
description: Find the volume of a cylinder using V = π × r² × h. Enter the radius and height in centimeters to get the result in cubic centimeters and liters.
metaDescription: Free cylinder volume calculator. Enter radius and height in cm to get the volume in cm³ and liters using V = π × r² × h. Fast and accurate.

result.label: Volume
result.empty: Enter the radius and height to calculate the volume.

sections.heading: How the cylinder volume formula works
paragraphs: The volume of a cylinder is V = π × r² × h, where r is the radius of the circular base and h is the height. The base area π × r² is multiplied by the height to fill the solid.
Because the radius is squared, doubling the radius makes the volume four times larger, while doubling the height only doubles it.

sections.heading: From cm³ to liters
paragraphs: One liter equals 1000 cm³, so the calculator divides the cubic-centimeter result by 1000 to give liters. This is handy for tanks, bottles, and containers.

howTo.name: Enter the radius
howTo.text: Type the radius of the circular base in centimeters.

howTo.name: Enter the height
howTo.text: Type the height of the cylinder in centimeters.

howTo.name: Read the result
howTo.text: The volume appears instantly in cm³ and liters.

faq.q: What is the formula for cylinder volume?
faq.a: V = π × r² × h, where r is the base radius and h is the height.

faq.q: What units does this calculator use?
faq.a: Enter the radius and height in centimeters; the result is shown in cm³ and liters.

faq.q: How do I convert cm³ to liters?
faq.a: Divide the volume in cm³ by 1000, since 1 liter equals 1000 cm³.

faq.q: Do I use the radius or the diameter?
faq.a: Use the radius. If you only have the diameter, divide it by 2 first.
Yes, it is completely free and works directly in your browser.
